01_initial_request.input_data: G12-scFv protein and P17 peptide tag
01_initial_request.user_intent: To enhance the therapeutic efficacy of G12-scFv against HBV by improving its stability, solubility, and hepatocyte-specific delivery
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Increased thermostability, antigen-binding affinity, expression solubility, and hepatocyte uptake efficiency

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] G12-scFv targeting HBV surface protein was previously generated but has low solubility. [Gap] Need to fuse P17 tag to improve solubility and hepatocyte targeting. [Decision] To achieve P17-tagged G12-scFv, chose to use PCR and Cloning to insert P17 tag coding sequence into G12-scFv plasmid, expecting to obtain pET28a-His-G12-scFv-HA-P17 plasmid.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Plasmid pET28a-His-G12-scFv-HA-P17 constructed encoding G12-scFv with C-terminal P17 tag.

references.citation: Wang et al., 2016 (Ref 34); Li et al., 2019 (Ref 18)
references.relevance: Describes the parental G12-scFv and plasmid construction strategies.

references.citation: Wong et al., 2006 (Ref 31); Sokoloff et al., 2003 (Ref 32); Ludtke et al., 2009 (Ref 33)
references.relevance: Describes P17 peptide hepatocyte targeting capability.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Plasmid constructed. [Gap] Need to produce protein to test solubility. [Decision] To achieve protein production, chose to use E. coli SHuffle T7 strain to express recombinant G12-scFv-HA-P17, expecting to obtain soluble protein due to oxidative cytoplasm.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: G12-scFv-HA-P17 protein expressed in E. coli SHuffle T7 cells.

references.citation: Lobstein et al., 2012 (Ref 19); Li et al., 2019 (Ref 18)
references.relevance: SHuffle strain supports disulfide bond formation in cytoplasm.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Protein expressed. [Gap] Need to quantify solubility enhancement. [Decision] To achieve solubility quantification, chose to use Western Blotting to detect total and soluble fractions, expecting to obtain solubility percentage.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Solubility of G12-scFv-HA-P17 was 45.0%, compared to 14.9% for untagged G12-scFv-HA.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Solubility enhanced in SHuffle. [Gap] Need to verify dependence on oxidative environment. [Decision] To achieve control verification, chose to use E. coli BL21(DE3) Star strain to express G12-scFv-HA-P17, expecting to observe insolubility due to reducing cytoplasm.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: G12-scFv-HA-P17 remained insoluble in BL21(DE3) Star strain.

references.relevance: BL21 cytoplasm lacks components to catalyze disulfide bonds.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Control expression done. [Gap] Need to confirm solubility in control strain. [Decision] To achieve solubility check, chose to use Western Blotting to detect fractions from BL21, expecting to confirm insolubility.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Solubility below detection limit in BL21 strain.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] G12-scFv solubility improved. [Gap] Need to test generalizability to other scFvs. [Decision] To achieve broad validation, chose to use PCR and Cloning to construct VRC01, ADRI, MA18/7 scFvs with P17 tag, expecting to obtain expression plasmids.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Plasmids for three additional scFvs with P17 tag constructed.

references.citation: Wu et al., 2010 (Ref 35); Cerino et al., 2015 (Ref 36); Kuttner et al., 1999 (Ref 37)
references.relevance: Sources of VRC01, ADRI, and MA18/7 scFv sequences.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Other scFv plasmids constructed. [Gap] Need to measure their solubility. [Decision] To achieve solubility measurement, chose to use Western Blotting to analyze soluble/total fractions, expecting to observe solubility enhancement.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: P17 tag increased solubility of other scFvs from 2.4 to 11.6-fold.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] C-terminal fusion works. [Gap] Need to test N-terminal fusion efficiency. [Decision] To achieve position optimization, chose to use PCR and Cloning to attach P17 tag to N-terminus of MA18/7-scFv, expecting to obtain N-terminal fusion plasmid.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: pET28a-His-P17-MA18/7-scFv-HA plasmid constructed.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] N-terminal construct ready. [Gap] Need to compare solubility with C-terminal. [Decision] To achieve comparison, chose to use Western Blotting to determine solubility of N- and C-terminal fusions, expecting to find both efficient.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: P17 tag improved solubility at both N- (11.6-fold) and C-positions (10.2-fold).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] P17 tag effective. [Gap] Need to compare with standard solubility tags. [Decision] To achieve benchmarking, chose to use PCR and Cloning to fuse MBP and GST tags to G12-scFv, expecting to obtain comparator constructs.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: MBP-G12-scFv and GST-G12-scFv-HA plasmids constructed.

references.citation: Bach et al., 2001 (Ref 27); Dyson et al., 2004 (Ref 39)
references.relevance: MBP and GST are common solubility tags.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Comparator constructs ready. [Gap] Need to evaluate their solubility enhancement. [Decision] To achieve evaluation, chose to use Western Blotting to measure solubility of MBP/GST fusions, expecting to compare with P17.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: MBP increased solubility by 1.4-fold; GST decreased solubility. P17 (1.7-fold) was more efficient.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Single P17 tag works. [Gap] Hypothesis that multiple tags might recruit more chaperones. [Decision] To achieve hypothesis testing, chose to use PCR and Cloning to fuse two or three copies of P17 tag, expecting to obtain multi-copy constructs.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: pET28a-His-MA18/7-scFv-2xP17 and 3xP17 plasmids constructed.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Multi-copy constructs ready. [Gap] Need to test solubility effect. [Decision] To achieve testing, chose to use Western Blotting to measure solubility of multi-copy fusions, expecting to see if enhancement increases.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Two and three copies enhanced solubility by only 6.6- and 3.8-fold, respectively (less than single copy).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Mechanism of solubility enhancement unknown. [Gap] Need to identify structural features of P17 tag. [Decision] To achieve structure prediction, chose to use JPred4 to predict secondary structure of P17 peptide, expecting to identify alpha-helix propensity.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Predicted a single region (residues 22-29) with alpha-helix propensity.

references.relevance: JPred4 algorithm for secondary structure prediction.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] JPred4 predicted helix. [Gap] Need 3D structural context. [Decision] To achieve 3D prediction, chose to use AlphaFold2 to predict structure of MA18/7-scFv-HA-P17, expecting to confirm helix in context.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: AlphaFold2 predicted local helix but with low confidence (pLDDT < 70).

references.relevance: AlphaFold2 for accurate protein structure prediction.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Helix predicted but low confidence. [Gap] Need to test if helix is required for function. [Decision] To achieve functional test, chose to use Site-directed mutagenesis to substitute A25 with Proline (A25P), expecting to disrupt helix.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: P17-A25P mutant plasmid constructed.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] A25P mutant constructed. [Gap] Need to measure solubility effect. [Decision] To achieve measurement, chose to use Western Blotting to compare solubility of WT and A25P, expecting to see if helix is required.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: A25P mutant increased solubility by around fourfold, similar to WT. Helix is dispensable.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Helix not required. [Gap] Need to identify sequence determinants. [Decision] To achieve sequence mapping, chose to use PCR and Cloning to generate N- and C-terminal truncation mutants of P17 tag, expecting to obtain deletion variants.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Truncated P17 tag plasmids constructed.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Truncation variants ready. [Gap] Need to test solubility enhancement. [Decision] To achieve testing, chose to use Western Blotting to measure solubility of truncation mutants, expecting to identify minimal effective sequence.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: P17-N18del (15 aa) promoted solubility as strongly as WT tag (8.5-fold).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Hydrophilic residues suspected to contribute. [Gap] Need to test role of charged residues. [Decision] To achieve mechanistic insight, chose to use Site-directed mutagenesis to replace charged residues (D, E, K, R) with Alanine, expecting to obtain charge mutants.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Charge mutant plasmids constructed.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Charge mutants ready. [Gap] Need to measure solubility impact. [Decision] To achieve measurement, chose to use Western Blotting to compare solubility of charge mutants, expecting to see reduced enhancement if charges are critical.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Solubility enhancement reduced by 12% to 58% in charge mutants. Charged residues have strongest impact.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Solubility enhanced. [Gap] Need to check thermostability. [Decision] To achieve stability measurement, chose to use Thermal Shift Assay with SYPRO Orange to determine Tm, expecting to observe increased Tm.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: G12-scFv-HA-P17 displayed 2.3°C higher Tm than G12-scFv-HA.

references.relevance: Thermal shift assay protocol for protein stability.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Stability increased. [Gap] Need to check antigen binding affinity. [Decision] To achieve affinity measurement, chose to use Biolayer Interferometry to determine Kd, expecting to observe improved affinity.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: G12-scFv-HA-P17 had 2.2-fold lower Kd (0.79x10^-10 M) than untagged version.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Affinity improved. [Gap] Need to verify specificity and binding in sera. [Decision] To achieve specificity check, chose to use ELISA with patient sera, expecting to observe higher signal with HBsAg positive sera.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: G12-scFv-HA-P17 showed twofold higher A450nm with HBsAg positive sera and lower with negative sera.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Binding improved. [Gap] Need to test functional neutralization of HBV. [Decision] To achieve functional validation, chose to use HBV Neutralization Assay in HepG2-NTCP cells, expecting to observe lower IC50.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: G12-scFv-HA-P17 displayed higher neutralizing activity (IC50 2.91 nM) than untagged (6.74 nM).

references.relevance: HBV production and neutralization assay protocol.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] P17 protein can oligomerize. [Gap] Need to ensure tag doesn't induce scFv oligomerization. [Decision] To achieve quaternary structure check, chose to use Size-Exclusion Chromatography to analyze G12-scFv-HA-P17, expecting to confirm monomeric/dimeric state.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: P17 tag did not induce oligomerization; monomer/dimer ratio same as untagged.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Comprehensive biochemical and functional assays (Western Blot, Thermal Shift, BLI, ELISA, Neutralization, SEC)

solubility_increase.interpretation: G12-scFv-P17 solubility increased from 14.9% to 45.0% (3.0-fold); up to 11.6-fold for MA18/7-scFv.

thermostability_increase.interpretation: Melting temperature (Tm) increased by 2.3°C, comparable to intradomain disulfide bonds.

binding_affinity_Kd.interpretation: Equilibrium dissociation constant improved 2.2-fold compared to untagged (1.77e-10 M).

neutralization_IC50.interpretation: 50% inhibitory concentration improved compared to untagged (6.74 nM).

hepatocyte_uptake.interpretation: P17-tagged scFv taken up more efficiently by primary mouse hepatocytes (Supplementary Fig. S6).

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective was achieved. The P17 tag significantly enhanced solubility, thermostability, antigen-binding affinity, and HBV-neutralizing activity of G12-scFv, while maintaining monomeric state and enabling hepatocyte targeting.
